verifier.py line 233: passed = score >= 75; README.md line 119: Pass threshold 75%

privileged_info_summary: The canvas_resize@1 task uses a real Unsplash landscape photo (800×600 pixels) that is downloaded from a public URL. The task verifies that agents can expand the canvas to exactly 1000×800 pixels (±5 pixel tolerance) while preserving the original image content and centering it properly. The verification checks four criteria: correct final dimensions (1000×800), significant area expansion (minimum 1.4× or 40% increase; actual is 1.67×), content preservation (mean pixel difference < 10), and proper centering (original content at offset 100,100). All four criteria must be met to pass (75% score threshold). The data is real (Unsplash), not synthetic by default, though a fallback synthetic image (800×600 solid blue with text) is created if the Unsplash download fails. Key numeric claims verified: original dimensions 800×600, target 1000×800, tolerance ±5 pixels, area ratio ≥1.4, content threshold <10, background variance <100, pass threshold ≥75%.
